wikitext:
{{Event Header|StS2 Trial.png}} {{E|The Trial|2}} is an {{2|Events|Event}} found exclusively in {{2|Glory}}. == Options == One of three trials is randomly selected. Each trial presents two verdicts: === Merchant Trial === {{Frame|DECIDE: Guilty}} {{TS|negative||Add {{C|Regret||2}} (curse) to your Deck.}} {{TS|positive||Obtain 2 random {{2|Relics|Relics}}.}}
{{Frame|DECIDE: Innocent}} {{TS|negative||Add {{C|Shame||2}} (curse) to your Deck.}} {{TS|positive||{{2|Upgrade}} 2 cards.}} === Noble Trial === {{Frame|DECIDE: Guilty}} {{TS|positive||Heal 10 HP.}}
{{Frame|DECIDE: Innocent}} {{TS|negative||Add {{C|Regret||2}} (curse) to your Deck.}} {{TS|positive||Gain 300 Gold.}} === Nondescript Trial === {{Frame|DECIDE: Guilty}} {{TS|negative||Add {{C|Doubt||2}} (curse) to your Deck.}} {{TS|positive||Gain 2 card rewards.}}
{{Frame|DECIDE: Innocent}} {{TS|negative||Add {{C|Doubt||2}} (curse) to your Deck.}} {{TS|positive||{{2|Transform}} 2 cards.}} == Dialogue ==
You join a line of people entering a massive building. As you pass under a {{TS|gold||golden archway}}, horns blare, {{TS||shake|confetti explodes}}, and streamers glide down from the ceiling! "Entrant {{TS|blue||[number]}}, you are the {{TS|green||DECIDER}} for today's {{TS|gold||Trial}}."
{{Frame|Reject}}
{{TS||shake|"YOU CANNOT REFUSE!!"}} Shouts the {{TS|gold||Grand Arbiter of the Courts}}. {{TS||shake|"Those who reject this privilege are penalized with}} {{TS|red|shake|DEATH}} {{TS||shake|Do you still defy!?"}}
{{Frame|Merchant Trial}}
A wealthy-looking merchant, presents his case. He is accused of murder by one of his rivals. The evidence is thin, and you have a feeling that the man is innocent, but the onlookers seem out for blood.
{{Frame|Merchant — Guilty}}
You rule that all of the man's possessions are to be taken and given as processing fees for the court. The crowd howls in glee!
{{Frame|Merchant — Innocent}}
You go with your conscience and rule that the man is innocent. Despite the boos and accusations of being a "paid-off judge" you feel like you did the right thing.
{{Frame|Noble Trial}}
A powerful noble steps forward. You find a note in your pocket indicating that if you decide innocence you will be handsomely rewarded. The noble barely argues against the corruption charges and gives you a knowing smirk. You can't stop corruption but you can pocket some gold...
{{Frame|Noble — Guilty}}
The powerful noble was beheaded. One less corrupt noble.
{{Frame|Noble — Innocent}}
You don't really know that he's guilty... Yeah, that's it! The bribe has nothing to do with the outcome here. Nope.
{{Frame|Nondescript Trial}}
A nondescript woman with a hard-to-read face steps forward. She is charged with the crime of theft and con-artistry. When the woman speaks, you believe her, but ample witnesses attest to her misdeeds. Both sides of the story are compelling!
{{Frame|Nondescript — Guilty}}
You aren't totally sure, but making the hard choice, you think the evidence is adequate. Did you really make the right call?
{{Frame|Nondescript — Innocent}}
You aren't totally sure, but you have a reasonable doubt. You don't know if you made the right call, but innocence seems like the better choice.
